assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and recognized as an intragroup monetary receivable/payable.

The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation because the group entities involved have different functional currencies.
The analysis is for consolidated financial statements rather than separate or individual financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognized intragroup dividend receivable/payable can be considered under the IFRS 9 exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats hedges of variability in cash flows of a recognized asset or liability as a separate hedge accounting model, so it is a distinct candidate treatment for foreign currency settlement risk.
